cand_pune_1.description: Led the city during the COVID-19 pandemic and oversaw infrastructure projects like the Pune Metro.
Managed the municipal corporation's budget and fiscal policies.

cand_pune_2.description: Won the high-profile Kasba Peth by-election, focusing on urban redevelopment.
Five-time municipal councilor known for local community outreach.

cand_pune_3.description: Led the MNS party in the Pune Municipal Corporation before joining VBA.

cand_var_1.description: Incumbent Prime Minister leading the NDA government.
Longest-serving CM of Gujarat, focused on economic development.

cand_var_2.description: Leading the Congress party in Uttar Pradesh.
Represented the Pindra assembly seat multiple times.

cand_nag_5.description: Prominent campaigner against corruption, notably exposing irregularities in Maharashtra's irrigation projects.
Contested the general elections from Nagpur as an AAP candidate, focusing on transparency and accountability.
Led the Aam Aadmi Party's organizational setup and campaigns in Maharashtra.

cand_mum_1.description: Two-time MP from Mumbai South, known for labor union leadership.
Served briefly as a Union Minister.

cand_mum_5.description: Contested the general elections on an Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) ticket, advocating for urban governance reforms.
Contested as an Independent candidate, focusing on transparency and civic issues in the aftermath of 26/11.

cand_mum_5.role: CEO, Royal Bank of Scotland India
cand_mum_5.description: Served as the CEO and Chairperson of RBS India, leading the bank's operations in the country.

cand_ame_2.description: Held portfolios for HRD, Textiles, and Women & Child Development.
Elected to Rajya Sabha (2011) and Lok Sabha (2019) from Amethi.

cand_blr_1.description: Three-time MP from Bangalore Central, focusing on urban infrastructure.

cand_nas_1.description: Elected MP from Nashik Lok Sabha constituency in the 2024 general elections on a Shiv Sena (UBT) ticket.
Served as MLA from Sinnar assembly constituency in Nashik district.
Third-generation politician. Grandfather Shankar Balaji Waje was the first MLA of Sinnar after Maharashtra's formation. Grandmother Rukminibai Waje was also an MLA from Sinnar.

cand_nas_2.description: Two-term MP from Nashik. First elected in 2014 defeating veteran NCP leader Chhagan Bhujbal, re-elected in 2019.
Served as municipal councillor in Nashik before contesting the Lok Sabha elections.
District-level representative, focusing on local development and rural infrastructure.
Served on the Senate of Pune University.

cand_nas_3.description: Contested Nashik Lok Sabha seat as an Independent after being denied a Shiv Sena (Shinde) ticket.

cand_nas_3.role: Lok Sabha Candidate, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar
cand_nas_3.description: Contested from Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar constituency and received over 1.48 lakh votes.
Head of the Janardan Swami Ashram near Ellora caves. Runs de-addiction centres, high schools, and colleges for social welfare.

cand_nas_4.description: Serves as the Vanchit Bahujan Aghadi district president for Nashik, building grassroots support for the party.
Contested the Nashik Lok Sabha seat in the 2019 general elections on a VBA ticket.
Professional background in agriculture and civil contracting in the Nashik region.

cand_nas_5.description: Won the Chandwad assembly seat for a third consecutive term in the 2024 Maharashtra Assembly Elections.
Re-elected from Chandwad, continuing development work in the constituency.
First elected to the Maharashtra Legislative Assembly from Chandwad on a BJP ticket.
Son of late Dr. Daulatrao Aher, former Maharashtra Health Minister and BJP MLA. Carries forward the family's political legacy in Nashik district.

cand_csn_1.role: Member of Parliament, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar
cand_csn_1.description: Won the Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar (Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar) Lok Sabha seat in 2024 on a Shiv Sena (Mahayuti) ticket.
Served as Cabinet Minister holding portfolios for Employment Guarantee and Horticulture. Also served as Guardian Minister for Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar district.
Elected from Paithan assembly constituency six times — in 1995, 1999, 2004, 2014, 2019, and 2024. One of the longest-serving MLAs in Marathwada.

cand_csn_2.role: Member of Parliament, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar
cand_csn_2.description: Elected to the Lok Sabha from Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar constituency, focusing on infrastructure and development.
Successfully transitioned from journalism to politics, winning the assembly seat for AIMIM.
Spent nearly 20 years in journalism, including 12 years with NDTV and 11 years with Lokmat.

cand_csn_3.role: Member of Parliament, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar
cand_csn_3.description: Represented Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar for four consecutive terms in the Lok Sabha, serving for 20 years.
Held portfolios for Housing, Transport, and Forests/Environment in the state government.
Served two terms in the Maharashtra Legislative Assembly before moving to national politics.
Led the opposition in the Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar Municipal Corporation as a corporator.

cand_csn_4.description: Re-elected to the state assembly as a Shiv Sena candidate.
First elected as an MLA representing the Maharashtra Navnirman Sena (MNS).
Son of late Raibhan Jadhav (former MLA) and son-in-law of Raosaheb Danve (former Union Minister).

cand_csn_5.description: Appointed as the official spokesperson for the Bharatiya Janata Party in the Marathwada region.
Served as the Deputy Mayor of the Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar Municipal Corporation, focusing on urban infrastructure and public grievances.
Led the BJP group in the municipal corporation, advocating for transparent governance and civic improvements.
